message:
beads: sync issue state for ft-xbnl0.5.1 and ft-xbnl0.5.3

Routine beads-to-JSONL flush covering two small state changes from
this work session:

- ft-xbnl0.5.3 moves from in_progress to closed, with close_reason
  pointing at docs/ft-xbnl0-5-3-blessed-tuning-validation.json and
  the new artifact bundle at
  tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.5.3/blessed_tuning_profiles/
  20260419T193608Z.  This matches the "codify blessed tuning
  profiles" commit that just landed.
- ft-xbnl0.5.1 updated_at bumps to 2026-04-19T19:45:19Z; no content
  change, just a touch from a concurrent agent as it claimed the docs
  slice.

No other issues touched.

files.patch:
@@ -108,7 +108,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","title":"Build the long-haul 50, 100, and 200-pane swarm soak harness and benchmark matrix","description":"# Goal
Create the workload harness that proves FrankenTerm can actually handle the large multi-agent operating model it was built for.

This task exists because the finish line is not satisfied by unit tests and short integration runs. The project needs a representative long-haul harness that exercises the control plane, mux, scrollback, search, and operator paths under realistic swarm pressure.","design":"# Design
1. Define representative 50, 100, and 200-pane workload profiles, including idle periods, bursty output, reconnects, search, capture, and policy-sensitive actions.
2. Build a soak harness that supports both shorter developer-smoke runs and release-scale long-haul runs so the workload definitions get exercised regularly instead of only at release time.
3. Record benchmark-style metrics and artifacts during the soak so later gates can reason about trend lines rather than a single pass fail.
4. Keep the harness operator-readable, segmentable, and repeatable enough for future release cycles and incident follow-up.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A repeatable soak harness exists for representative multi-agent workloads at 50, 100, and 200 pane scale.
2. The harness records enough metrics and artifacts to diagnose failures without local guesswork.
3. The harness is tied to concrete performance and stability expectations rather than being a best-effort stress script.
4. Completion evidence includes at least one representative run record and artifact bundle layout.
5. The harness includes a shorter smoke profile and a release-scale long-haul profile so the same workload model is usable in both frequent validation and deep proof runs.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the bead that transforms the user’s original mission statement into an executable test campaign.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:02:07.347208Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:02:07.347037Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","performance","reliability","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.148984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.506745Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.420840Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.729476Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:30.976172Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.086328Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","title":"Turn runtime SLOs, soak confidence, and performance budgets into hard release gates","description":"# Goal
Convert reliability and performance evidence into explicit release gates.

This task exists because data that is not attached to a gate will be ignored the next time the schedule gets tight. The finish-line story needs hard go or no-go criteria for stability, leak behavior, and performance budgets.","design":"# Design
1. Define the SLO and budget thresholds that matter for the finish line.
2. Connect those thresholds to the instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak outputs from earlier beads.
3. Make failure modes explicit and actionable so the gates are useful rather than ceremonial.
4. Record the resulting gate definitions in bead-native form for release work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reliability and performance budgets are explicit and machine-checkable or procedure-checkable.
2. The release process has named gates for leak behavior, soak confidence, and relevant performance metrics.
3. Gate failures produce actionable diagnostics.
4. Release-readiness tasks can rely on these gates rather than reinventing them.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is what prevents multi-day swarm reliability from devolving back into a vibes-based claim.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:21:34.302865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:21:34.302591Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","release-readiness","reliability","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:41.955060Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.766069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.417607Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.385704Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5","title":"[EPIC] Release readiness, operator polish, and permanent regression guards","description":"# Goal
Turn the completed technical work into a ship-ready, operator-trustworthy project: final docs, guards, tuning guidance, end-to-end acceptance scenarios, and a closure bundle that future contributors can rely on.

This epic exists because a project can be technically improved and still not be finish-line ready if docs lie, CI does not defend the invariants, or no one has captured the evidence and workflows that define ready for real multi-agent use.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the narrative around the final native architecture and honest supported behavior.
2. Add permanent machine checks for the invariants this program establishes.
3. Expose live health and incident-diagnostics surfaces so operators can actually use the reliability work under pressure.
4. Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints so the finished system is operable from the first real interaction.
5. Validate the original mission workflows end to end, not just unit by unit.
6. Package the result into a final closure bundle with explicit remaining risks, if any.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Docs and operator guidance describe the final system rather than a migration-in-progress story.
2. CI, doctor, and audit surfaces fail fast when `tokio`, `smol`, or fake supported capabilities creep back in.
3. Operator acceptance scenarios covering the original mission are executed with artifacts.
4. A final release-candidate checklist and evidence bundle exist in bead-native form.
5. Operator-facing health and diagnostic workflows are first-class outputs of this epic, not incidental notes.
6. First-run setup and recovery entrypoints are polished enough to be part of the supported operator story rather than an undocumented initiation ritual.","notes":"This epic is what prevents the repo from sliding back into technically interesting but operationally ambiguous. It closes the loop between implementation, proof, and operator trust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:09:15.048719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","goal-line","operator","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.840501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","title":"Rewrite README, AGENTS, and operator docs around the final native Asupersync architecture and honest support matrix","description":"# Goal
Make the repository narrative match the finished system.

This task exists because docs that still describe migration debt, placeholder capabilities, or outdated runtime assumptions will immediately undermine the credibility of the finish-line push.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the high-level architecture story around the final native Asupersync model.
2. Update AGENTS guidance and operator docs to reflect the real supported capability matrix, verification expectations, and live health or diagnostic surfaces added by this program.
3. Remove or rewrite stale references to transitional architecture that no longer exists.
4. Keep the docs specific enough that future agents can work from them without reopening old planning documents.","acceptance_criteria":"1. README, AGENTS, and core operator docs describe the final system rather than a migration in progress.
2. Runtime, support-matrix, and verification language is accurate and current.
3. Operators and future agents can understand the intended architecture and supported surfaces directly from the docs.
4. The updated docs are cross-linked to the finish-line evidence model where appropriate.
5. Documentation examples, support claims, and operator procedures are validated against the shipped commands, recorded verification artifacts, live diagnostic surfaces, and the first-run or recovery path so the docs cannot drift ahead of reality.","notes":"This bead is the documentation counterpart to the code cleanup. If the narrative stays stale, the project will feel unfinished even after the code improves.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:38:33.648122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.535955Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.715747Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.022385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.471912Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:09.776559Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:14.259339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","title":"Add permanent CI, doctor, and audit guards for zero tokio smol and zero fake supported capabilities","description":"# Goal
Make the finish-line invariants self-defending.

This task exists because the project needs durable protection against two classes of regression: runtime debt creeping back in and fake supported capabilities quietly reappearing.","design":"# Design
1. Add or extend CI checks, doctor surfaces, and audit scripts that defend the no-Tokio no-Smol property and the honest support-matrix property.
2. Keep the checks actionable so failures explain what violated the rule and where.
3. Wire the checks into the normal contributor path instead of leaving them as optional extras.
4. Reuse the evidence and inventories built earlier in the program where possible.
5. Validate not only code invariants but also the verification contract itself where practical, including required artifact manifests, log schema, and deterministic E2E entrypoints.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The key finish-line invariants are defended by permanent automated or semi-automated checks.
2. Contributors can run the relevant checks locally and in CI.
3. Violations are specific enough to fix quickly.
4. The release lane can trust these guards as part of ongoing maintenance.
5. Selected deterministic E2E scripts, artifact manifests, and structured-log expectations are automatically checked or linted so the testing and evidence discipline in this program does not silently decay.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is how the finish-line work stays finished after the first release candidate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341498Z","close_reason":"finish-line guards passing; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","doctor","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.793483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.811867Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.145075Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","title":"Define blessed tuning profiles and operator playbooks for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane fleets","description":"# Goal
Turn the performance and reliability findings into concrete operating guidance.

This task exists because a finished swarm terminal should not leave operators guessing which settings, limits, and practices are appropriate for small, medium, and very large fleets.","design":"# Design
1. Derive tuning profiles from the reliability and soak evidence instead of speculation.
2. Document the practical knobs, warning signs, diagnostic surfaces, and recommended operating patterns for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane deployments.
3. Keep the guidance honest about which workloads are supported and which are still aspirational.
4. Package the result so future operators can use it without reading the whole backlog.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has concrete tuning guidance for representative small, medium, and large fleet sizes.
2. The guidance is tied to measured evidence rather than vague rules of thumb.
3. Operators can use the playbooks and diagnostic surfaces to configure and monitor the system responsibly.
4. The playbooks are integrated with the final docs lane.
5. The playbooks cite exact verification scripts, thresholds, logged artifacts, and escalation paths instead of vague advice.","notes":"This bead turns performance work into something that operators can actually act on.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844353Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete; artifacts docs/ft-xbnl0-5-3-blessed-tuning-validation.json and tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.5.3/blessed_tuning_profiles/20260419T193608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","operator","performance","tuning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.072933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.582153Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:10.265504Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3556,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyTiger claiming docs/playbook slice: blessed fleet profiles + operator guidance docs tied to 4.6 gate artifacts; avoiding benchmark/runtime implementation surfaces.","created_at":"2026-04-19T19:34:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","title":"Run original-mission operator acceptance scenarios from fresh install through long-haul recovery","description":"# Goal
Prove the original user mission in end-to-end operator terms.

This task exists because the finish line is not a set of isolated unit victories. It is a story that starts from setup, moves through normal multi-agent operation, survives errors and disconnects, and still looks trustworthy after long-haul use.","design":"# Design
1. Define acceptance scenarios that cover clean setup, environment validation, normal swarm operation, pane search and control, failure recovery, restore, incident triage, and long-haul stability checks.
2. Run those scenarios against the system with the final guardrails, setup or recovery polish, and tuning guidance in place.
3. Capture artifacts that make the result auditable by someone who did not watch the run live.
4. Record exactly what still feels rough or unsupported if any scenario falls short.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has end-to-end operator acceptance scenarios that directly represent the original mission.
2. Each scenario has stored artifacts and exact run procedures.
3. Success and failure criteria are explicit enough that future release candidates can repeat them.
4. Remaining gaps, if any, are documented honestly rather than hidden in a success narrative.
5. The scenario set explicitly covers clean setup, broken-environment diagnosis, standard incident triage through the new diagnostic surfaces, and recovery entry back to steady-state operation.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the human and operator counterpart to the soak and deterministic reliability lanes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.189038Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:16.119166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acceptance","e2e","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.332257Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.035141Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.892317Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:45.331092Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.189038Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:45.786551Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.265550Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:10.762074Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:13.106214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
